rulings.Case Title: ALLEN KULIKOFF VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 23, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz, Edelman & Dicker¿s motion to be relieved as counsel for Stone Construction Equipment, Inc. is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CLINTON BOGAN JR. et al VS. AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORPORATION (SUED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MAY 23, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether the Cranite sheet gaskets to which Mr. Bogan was exposed were attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ objections to Mr. Pantaleoni¿s deposition testimony are overruled. Plaintiffs¿ objections to Crane Co.¿s interrogatory responses are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CLINTON BOGAN JR. et al VS. AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORPORATION (SUED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MAY 23, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Velan Valve Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (negligence), 2 (strict liability and 5 (loss of consortium) and granted as to issues 3 (false representation), 4 (intentional tort/intentional failure to warn) and 6 (punitive damages). As to summary judgment and adjudication issues 1, 2 and 5, defendant sustained its initial burden of production. The deposition testimony of Mr. Bogan, Mr. Velan and Mr. Francois create a triable issue whether Mr. Bogan was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (gaskets) attributable to defendant. As to false representation, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant made a false representation. As to intentional tort/failure to warn, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue on this cause of action. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ San Diego Watercrafts objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Velan and Mr. Francois are overruled and plaintiff¿s objections to the declarations of Admiral Horne and Mr. Francois are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JUDITH SHRUM et al VS. 3M COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Claim And Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MAY 23, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant Hajoca Corp.¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs¿ complaint relating to punitive damages is off calendar as moot given the ruling on defendant¿s demurrer. =(503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JUDITH SHRUM et al VS. 3M COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MAY 23, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant Hajoca Corp.¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Although not cited by defendant, Bockrath is the controlling authority. Defendant¿s demurrer as to the successor-in-interest allegations is overruled. Plaintiffs¿ complaint pleads sufficient allegations pertaining to successor-in-interest liability. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE MAHER VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MAY 23, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Kerr Corp.¿s special demurrer to the first and second causes of action of plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiff an opportunity, if he is able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Defendant¿s special demurrer to the third cause of action of plaintiff¿s complaint is overruled. Plaintiff¿s complaint pleads facts sufficient to state a cause of action for premises owner/contractor liability. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court.
